issue_description: The documentation for elementSiblingIndex states "Get the list index of this element in its element sibling list. I.e. if this is the first element sibling, returns 0". This would imply that if n=myElem.elementSiblingIndex(); then myElem.parent().children().get(n)==myElem. However, this is not how elementSiblingIndex behaves. What is guaranteed is that myElem.parent().children().get(n).equals(myElem). For example, if both row 2 and row 5 of a table are Cell1Cell2 then the Element object associated with both rows will have the same elementSiblingIndex().
